question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, after a maker registers a Merkle root via setIsRootRatified for offers designed at a specific settlement fee level, take those offers after the feeSetter raises settlementFeeCbp[i] for the market, causing the taker (seller) to receive fewer sellerAssets than the maker intended, effectively charging the wrong side an unintended fee? Preconditions: maker registers root for sell offer at tick T with expected sellerPrice = tickToPrice(T); feeSetter calls setMarketSettlementFee to raise the fee; taker calls take() with valid Merkle proof. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, higherFee) -> take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, taker, ...). Invariant tested: sellerAssets received by maker-as-seller must equal units.mulDivUp(offerPrice, WAD) regardless of fee changes after root registration. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed from seller side; maker-as-seller receives less than intended. Proof idea: fuzz (tick, units, feeBeforeRegistration, feeAfterRegistration) and assert sellerAssets == units.mulDivUp(tickToPrice(tick), WAD) for sell offers.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, after a maker registers a Merkle root for buy offers at tick T, take those offers after the feeSetter lowers settlementFeeCbp[i] to zero, causing claimableSettlementFee to increase by zero (underclaim) while the maker-as-buyer still pays units.mulDivDown(offerPrice, WAD) and the seller receives the full offerPrice amount, resulting in zero protocol fee collected? Preconditions: market has non-zero settlement fee at root registration time; feeSetter sets fee to 0 before take; taker takes with valid proof. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, 0) -> take(buyOffer, ...). Invariant tested: claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] must increase by exactly buyerAssets - sellerAssets >= 0 on every take. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed (zero fee collected despite non-zero fee at offer creation time). Proof idea: unit test asserting claimableSettlementFee delta == 0 when fee is zeroed between registration and take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.settlementFee / Midnight.take] Can a taker exploit the piecewise linear interpolation truncation in settlementFee() by taking at a TTM that is exactly at a breakpoint boundary (e.g., TTM == 1 days, 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, 180 days) to cause the interpolated fee to differ from the breakpoint fee by up to (end - start - 1) / (end - start) of the fee delta, resulting in a systematically underclaimed settlement fee? Preconditions: market has non-zero fee at adjacent breakpoints with feeLower != feeUpper; taker times the take to land at a breakpoint boundary. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> warp(maturity - breakpointTTM) -> take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...). Invariant tested: settlementFee(id, TTM) must equal feeLower when TTM == start of interval and feeUpper when TTM == end of interval. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed due to integer division truncation at boundary. Proof idea: fuzz TTM across all 6 intervals and assert fee is within [feeLower, feeUpper] and equals breakpoint values exactly at boundaries.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight._updatePosition] Can a taker, by taking a buy offer where the buyer (maker) has no existing credit and units <= buyerPos.debt, bypass the _updatePosition call for the buyer (since hasCredit(id, buyer) == false and units <= buyerPos.debt), causing the buyer's continuous fee to not accrue for the elapsed time, resulting in continuousFeeCredit being understated relative to the actual time-weighted fee owed? Preconditions: buyer (maker) has existing debt >= units and zero credit; market has non-zero continuousFee; time has elapsed since lastAccrual. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, ..., units <= makerDebt) where maker has no credit. Invariant tested: continuousFeeCredit must increase by the accrued fee for all positions with credit whenever a take touches those positions. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed; protocol loses fee revenue. Proof idea: unit test comparing continuousFeeCredit before and after take when buyer has debt >= units and no credit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight._updatePosition] Can a taker, by taking a sell offer where the seller (maker) has no credit (pure borrower with only debt), bypass the _updatePosition call for the seller (since hasCredit(id, seller) == false), causing the seller's continuous fee to not accrue, and if the seller later gains credit via a subsequent take, the lastAccrual timestamp is stale, causing a fee accrual gap? Preconditions: seller (maker) has only debt and no credit; market has non-zero continuousFee; time has elapsed. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(sellOffer, ...) [seller has no credit] -> time passes -> take again [seller now has credit]. Invariant tested: every position with credit must have its fee accrued up to the current timestamp on every interaction. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed for the gap period. Proof idea: differential test comparing continuousFeeCredit with and without the intermediate no-credit take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.claimContinuousFee] Can the continuousFeeCredit for a market become permanently unclaimable if continuousFeeCredit > withdrawable, since claimContinuousFee requires amount <= withdrawable AND amount <= continuousFeeCredit, making any claim that would fully drain continuousFeeCredit impossible when withdrawable < continuousFeeCredit? Preconditions: market has accumulated continuousFeeCredit via multiple _updatePosition calls; withdrawable is reduced by withdraw() calls; continuousFeeCredit > withdrawable. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take (accrues fees) -> withdraw (reduces withdrawable) -> claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit, receiver) [reverts]. Invariant tested: claimableAmount = min(continuousFeeCredit, withdrawable, totalUnits) must always be >= continuousFeeCredit when no bad debt exists. Scoped impact: continuous fee unclaimable; protocol revenue frozen. Proof idea: fuzz (credit, feeRate, ttm, withdrawAmount) and assert claimContinuousFee(continuousFeeCredit) succeeds after repay restores withdrawable.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / Midnight.claimContinuousFee] Can bad debt realization via liquidate() cause continuousFeeCredit to be slashed proportionally (via the lossFactor update) while withdrawable is simultaneously increased by repaidUnits, creating a state where continuousFeeCredit < actual accrued fees that lenders owe, resulting in the protocol being unable to claim the full continuous fee that was economically earned? Preconditions: market has accumulated continuousFeeCredit; liquidation realizes bad debt (badDebt > 0); continuousFeeCredit is slashed by lossFactor update. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take (creates debt and credit) -> updatePosition (accrues fees) -> liquidate with badDebt > 0 -> claimContinuousFee. Invariant tested: sum of all accrued continuousFeeCredit must be claimable after any sequence of liquidations. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed after bad debt socialization. Proof idea: unit test asserting continuousFeeCredit after liquidation equals pre-liquidation value scaled by (totalUnits - badDebt) / totalUnits.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take (buy offer, mulDivDown rounding)] Can a taker, by choosing units such that units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) == units.mulDivDown(sellerPrice, WAD) (i.e., the rounding causes buyerAssets == sellerAssets), cause claimableSettlementFee to increase by zero despite a non-zero settlement fee rate, effectively making the settlement fee unclaimable for that trade? Preconditions: offer.buy == true; settlement fee is non-zero but small relative to units; units chosen so that mulDivDown rounding collapses the spread. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, ..., units=dustAmount). Invariant tested: if settlementFee(id, TTM) > 0 and units > 0, then buyerAssets - sellerAssets > 0. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed (zero fee on non-zero-fee trade). Proof idea: fuzz (units, tick, fee) and find units where mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) == mulDivDown(units, sellerPrice, WAD).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take (sell offer, mulDivUp rounding)] Can a taker, by choosing units such that units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD) - units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD) > units * settlementFee / WAD (i.e., rounding causes the fee to be overcollected), cause claimableSettlementFee to increase by more than the intended fee, effectively overclaiming from the buyer? Preconditions: offer.buy == false; settlement fee is non-zero; units chosen to maximize rounding divergence between mulDivUp(buyerPrice) and mulDivUp(sellerPrice). Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(sellOffer, ..., units=specificAmount). Invariant tested: buyerAssets - sellerAssets <= ceil(units * settlementFee / WAD). Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed from buyer side. Proof idea: fuzz (units, tick, fee) and assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets <= ceil(units * settlementFee / WAD).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee] Can a taker take a SetterRatifier-authorized offer at exactly TTM == 0 (post-maturity, block.timestamp == market.maturity) where timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(maturity, block.timestamp) = 0, causing settlementFee to use the cbp0 breakpoint (0d fee), and if cbp0 is set lower than cbp1, the fee is underclaimed relative to what the maker expected when registering the root? Preconditions: market maturity is in the past or at current block; cbp0 < cbp1; maker registered root expecting cbp1 fee. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> warp(market.maturity) -> take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...). Invariant tested: post-maturity takes must use cbp0 fee, and this must be consistent with maker's registered offer intent. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed at maturity boundary. Proof idea: unit test at block.timestamp == maturity asserting settlementFee(id, 0) == cbp0 * CBP.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.claimSettlementFee / claimableSettlementFee] Can the feeClaimer overclaim settlement fees by calling claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) where amount == claimableSettlementFee[token] which aggregates fees from ALL markets sharing the same loanToken, effectively allowing fees from one market to be claimed against the balance contributed by another market, causing the contract to transfer tokens that belong to lenders/borrowers of a different market? Preconditions: two markets share the same loanToken; market A has high claimableSettlementFee; market B has low actual token balance contribution. Call sequence: touchMarket(marketA) -> take(offerA, ...) -> touchMarket(marketB) -> take(offerB, ...) -> claimSettlementFee(loanToken, totalFees, receiver). Invariant tested: claimableSettlementFee[token] must never exceed sum of (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) across all takes for that token. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed; solvency invariant violated. Proof idea: invariant test asserting contract.balance[token] >= withdrawable[allMarkets] + claimableSettlementFee[token].

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / buyerPendingFeeIncrease] Can a taker cause an overflow in the continuous fee calculation buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) when continuousFee is at MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE (~317 per second) and timeToMaturity is near 100 * 365 days (max maturity), causing the product continuousFee * timeToMaturity to overflow uint256 before the mulDivDown, resulting in incorrect (zero or wrapped) pendingFee and thus underclaimed continuous fees? Preconditions: market has continuousFee == MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE; maturity is set to block.timestamp + 100 * 365 days; large buyerCreditIncrease. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, ..., units=MAX_CREDIT). Invariant tested: buyerPendingFeeIncrease must equal floor(buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * timeToMaturity / WAD) without overflow. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed due to arithmetic overflow. Proof idea: fuzz (continuousFee, timeToMaturity, creditIncrease) and check for overflow in continuousFee * timeToMaturity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / updatePositionView] Can a lender's continuous fee be overclaimed by the protocol if the sellerPendingFeeDecrease calculation uses mulDivUp(sellerPos.pendingFee, sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit) and rounds up to a value greater than the proportional share, causing the seller's pendingFee to decrease by more than the proportional amount, effectively charging the seller more continuous fee than they owe? Preconditions: seller has existing credit and pendingFee; sellerCreditDecrease is chosen to maximize mulDivUp rounding error. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(sellOffer, ..., units=specificAmount) where sellerCreditDecrease causes rounding. Invariant tested: sellerPendingFeeDecrease <= sellerPos.pendingFee * sellerCreditDecrease / sellerPos.credit (no overcollection). Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed from seller; seller's net credit understated. Proof idea: fuzz (pendingFee, credit, creditDecrease) and assert sellerPendingFeeDecrease <= pendingFee * creditDecrease / credit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.setMarketContinuousFee] Can a taker exploit the fact that the continuous fee rate is fixed per position at the time of credit creation (pendingFee is set at take time using _marketState.continuousFee), by taking a SetterRatifier-authorized offer after the feeSetter raises continuousFee, causing new lenders to pay a higher continuous fee than the maker intended when registering the root, effectively charging the wrong fee to the buyer-maker? Preconditions: maker registers root for buy offer expecting continuousFee = F; feeSetter raises continuousFee to F' > F; taker takes the offer. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> setMarketContinuousFee(id, higherFee) -> take(buyOffer, ...). Invariant tested: buyerPendingFeeIncrease must reflect the fee rate at the time of root registration, not the current rate. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed from buyer-maker. Proof idea: unit test comparing pendingFee increase with fee rate at registration vs. at take time.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.withdraw / Midnight.claimContinuousFee] Can a lender withdraw all their credit via withdraw() after continuous fees have accrued, causing withdrawable to decrease by the full credit amount (including the pendingFee portion), while continuousFeeCredit retains the accrued fee amount, making continuousFeeCredit > withdrawable and rendering the continuous fee permanently unclaimable? Preconditions: lender has credit with accrued pendingFee; lender calls withdraw(units == credit) after updatePosition; continuousFeeCredit > 0. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take (creates credit) -> updatePosition (accrues fee) -> withdraw(credit) -> claimContinuousFee(continuousFeeCredit) [reverts]. Invariant tested: after any sequence of withdrawals, claimContinuousFee(continuousFeeCredit) must succeed. Scoped impact: continuous fee unclaimable after full withdrawal. Proof idea: unit test asserting claimContinuousFee(continuousFeeCredit) succeeds after lender withdraws all credit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (interpolation)] Can a taker cause the settlement fee interpolation to return a value that is not monotonically increasing with TTM (i.e., fee at TTM=X > fee at TTM=X+1 within the same interval) due to integer division truncation in (feeLower * (end - timeToMaturity) + feeUpper * (timeToMaturity - start)) / (end - start), causing the fee to be underclaimed at certain TTM values within an interval? Preconditions: feeLower < feeUpper for a given interval; TTM chosen to maximize truncation error. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> warp(maturity - TTM) -> take(offer, ...). Invariant tested: settlementFee(id, TTM) is non-decreasing as TTM increases within any interval. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed at specific TTM values. Proof idea: fuzz TTM within each interval and assert fee(TTM) <= fee(TTM+1).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.multicall] Can a taker use multicall to atomically (1) call setIsRootRatified on behalf of an authorized delegate to register a root, and (2) immediately take an offer from that root, where the offer was crafted to exploit a fee boundary condition (e.g., TTM exactly at a breakpoint), causing the settlement fee to be computed at the lower breakpoint value rather than the higher one, underclaiming the fee? Preconditions: taker is authorized by maker; taker crafts offer with TTM at a breakpoint; taker uses multicall to register and take atomically. Call sequence: multicall([setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true), take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...)]). Invariant tested: fee computed in take() must use the correct breakpoint for the TTM at execution time. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed via atomic registration and take at fee boundary. Proof idea: unit test with multicall at TTM == 7 days asserting fee == cbp2 * CBP.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / continuousFeeCredit accounting] Can a taker cause continuousFeeCredit to exceed totalUnits by repeatedly taking offers that create credit with high pendingFee (near-maturity, high continuousFee), causing _updatePosition to add accruedFee to continuousFeeCredit while totalUnits grows more slowly, making claimContinuousFee(continuousFeeCredit) revert because amount > totalUnits? Preconditions: market near maturity with high continuousFee; multiple takes creating credit with large pendingFee; continuousFeeCredit accumulates faster than totalUnits. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> multiple take() calls -> updatePosition() -> claimContinuousFee(continuousFeeCredit) [reverts if continuousFeeCredit > totalUnits]. Invariant tested: continuousFeeCredit <= totalUnits at all times. Scoped impact: continuous fee unclaimable when continuousFeeCredit > totalUnits. Proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting continuousFeeCredit <= totalUnits after any sequence of takes and updatePositions.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / sellerPendingFeeDecrease] Can a taker, by taking a sell offer where the seller has credit == 1 and pendingFee == 1 and sellerCreditDecrease == 1, cause sellerPendingFeeDecrease = mulDivUp(1, 1, 1) = 1, which is correct, but if credit == 2 and pendingFee == 1 and sellerCreditDecrease == 1, cause sellerPendingFeeDecrease = mulDivUp(1, 1, 2) = 1 (rounds up from 0.5), overclaiming the continuous fee from the seller by 0.5 units? Preconditions: seller has small credit and pendingFee values; sellerCreditDecrease chosen to trigger mulDivUp rounding. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(sellOffer, ..., units=1). Invariant tested: sum of sellerPendingFeeDecrease across all partial takes must not exceed original pendingFee. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed from seller via rounding. Proof idea: unit test with credit=2, pendingFee=1, two sequential takes of 1 unit each, asserting total pendingFeeDecrease <= 1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / updatePositionView] Can a lender's continuous fee be underclaimed if _updatePosition is called at block.timestamp == market.maturity (accrualEnd = min(block.timestamp, maturity) = maturity), causing fee = pendingFee.mulDivDown(maturity - lastAccrual, maturity - lastAccrual) = pendingFee, which correctly accrues all remaining fees, but if lastAccrual == maturity (already accrued at maturity), fee = 0 and subsequent calls never accrue, leaving continuousFeeCredit understated? Preconditions: lender's lastAccrual is set to maturity in a prior call; time passes post-maturity; updatePosition called again. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take -> warp(maturity) -> updatePosition [sets lastAccrual = maturity] -> warp(maturity + 1) -> updatePosition [fee = 0]. Invariant tested: all pendingFee must be fully accrued to continuousFeeCredit by maturity. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed if accrual at maturity is missed. Proof idea: unit test asserting continuousFeeCredit == total expected fee after updatePosition at maturity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / claimableSettlementFee] Can a taker, by taking a zero-unit offer (units == 0) with a valid SetterRatifier proof, cause buyerAssets == sellerAssets == 0 and claimableSettlementFee to increase by 0, while still consuming offer capacity (consumed[maker][group] += 0), effectively wasting a take slot without contributing any fee, and if repeated, causing the offer to be fully consumed with zero fee collected? Preconditions: offer has maxUnits > 0; taker takes with units == 0 repeatedly; settlement fee is non-zero. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(offer, ..., units=0) * N times. Invariant tested: if units == 0, no state changes should occur (no fee, no credit, no debt). Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed (zero fee on zero-unit takes). Proof idea: unit test asserting all state variables unchanged after take with units == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (CBP precision)] Can a taker cause the settlement fee to be systematically underclaimed due to the CBP (1e12) precision loss when fees are stored as uint16 settlementFeeCbp values, where the actual fee is settlementFeeCbp * 1e12, causing any fee set between k*1e12 and (k+1)*1e12 to be rounded down to k*1e12, losing up to 1e12 - 1 WAD units per take? Preconditions: feeSetter sets a settlement fee that is not a multiple of CBP (1e12); market is created with this fee. Call sequence: setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, index, fee) where fee % CBP != 0 [reverts due to FeeNotMultipleOfFeeCbp check]. Invariant tested: all settlement fees must be multiples of CBP; no fee can be set between CBP multiples. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed due to precision loss. Proof idea: unit test asserting setDefaultSettlementFee reverts for any fee not divisible by CBP.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized delegate call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) to register a Merkle root containing offers with artificially low ticks (low price), then after the maker revokes the delegate's authorization via setIsAuthorized(delegate, false), the root remains ratified (isRootRatified[maker][root] == true), allowing takers to continue taking those low-price offers and paying a lower settlement fee than the current market rate, underclaiming fees? Preconditions: delegate is authorized by maker; delegate registers root with low-tick offers; maker revokes delegate authorization; root remains ratified. Call sequence: setIsAuthorized(delegate, true) -> setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> setIsAuthorized(delegate, false) -> take(lowTickOffer, ...). Invariant tested: revoking delegate authorization must invalidate all roots registered by that delegate. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed via stale root after authorization revocation. Proof idea: unit test asserting take succeeds with stale root after delegate deauthorization.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / buyerPendingFeeIncrease (timeToMaturity == 0)] Can a taker take a SetterRatifier-authorized buy offer at exactly TTM == 0 (post-maturity), causing buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * 0, WAD) = 0, so no continuous fee is charged to the buyer despite the buyer gaining credit, while the settlement fee uses cbp0 (potentially non-zero), resulting in continuous fee being underclaimed for post-maturity credit creation? Preconditions: block.timestamp >= market.maturity; offer.buy == true; market has non-zero continuousFee. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> warp(market.maturity) -> take(buyOffer, ...). Invariant tested: continuous fee must be zero for post-maturity credit creation (TTM == 0). Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed post-maturity (by design or bug). Proof idea: unit test asserting buyerPendingFeeIncrease == 0 when TTM == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / consumed tracking with maxAssets] Can a taker, when taking a buy offer with maxAssets set, cause the consumed tracking to use buyerAssets (which includes the settlement fee) rather than sellerAssets, meaning the offer cap is consumed faster than the actual credit created, effectively causing the offer to be exhausted before the maker's intended credit volume is reached, and the settlement fee is implicitly overcounted in the cap? Preconditions: offer.buy == true; offer.maxAssets > 0; settlement fee is non-zero. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, ..., units) where consumed[maker][group] += buyerAssets. Invariant tested: consumed tracking for buy offers with maxAssets must reflect the maker's intended volume (credit units or sellerAssets), not buyerAssets. Scoped impact: settlement fee overcounted in offer cap; maker's offer exhausted prematurely. Proof idea: fuzz (units, fee, maxAssets) and assert consumed == buyerAssets for buy offers with maxAssets.
